steps.title: Use Text Two's concern
steps.content: Text 1 reports that students who received entrepreneurial training showed a high likelihood of creating new businesses. Text 2 warns that studies of training often have methodological flaws, especially a lack of control groups and missing pretraining baselines. Those flaws matter because they make it hard to know whether the training caused the outcome.

steps.title: Test the objection
steps.content: Choice A raises participant awareness, which Text 2 never names. Choice C misuses sample size by asking about a smaller study rather than causation. Choice D introduces training length, another issue Text 2 does not mention. Choice B states the control-group objection: the participants may have shown the same business-creation likelihood even without training. The answer is B.
